headline: Anthropic's Mythos-class Claude Fable 5 anchors this cycle, framing a broader push toward agent infrastructure that holds context across long-running work.
body: The recommended channels lead with Anthropic's release of Claude Mythos (Fable 5), which tops SWE Bench Pro and Frontier Code and demonstrates vision, long-context, and coding capability [2], with coverage extending the model into regulated industries and a "Know Your Agent" context-engineering method for long-running applications [3]. Adjacent recommended work targets the memory and context gap directly: PAI builds a Claude Code Life OS giving agents persistent memory, custom skills, and a seven-phase observe-plan-build-verify-learn loop [0], while Open Notebook offers a self-hosted, API-driven NotebookLM alternative for grounded question-answering and podcast generation over custom sources [4]. IBM Technology covers the cost side of scaling agentic systems, where multi-agent coordination raises latency and failure risk [5], and the security side, where prompt injection, self-replicating AI worms, and nonhuman identity failures expose agents to manipulation [1]. Beyond the inner circle, Snorkel shows a 4B model finetuned with reinforcement learning and tool discipline beating Qwen 3 235B on structured queries [8], Gemma 4's 31B ranks fourth among open models under Apache 2.0 with on-device variants [10], and PostHog and Google Cloud demo self-correcting agent pipelines that turn product signals and ReAct planning into ready-to-merge pull requests [12][13].
